evidence: Fresh PoC links against builds/protobuf-asan-arm64/lib/libprotobuf.a and targets google::protobuf::json::JsonStringToMessage() with a large google.protobuf.Any JSON payload, but the ASan binary crashes before main() in google::protobuf::DescriptorPool::Tables::Tables() / InternalAddGeneratedFile(). A descriptor-only control exits 0, while an Any+JSON control without the oversized payload reproduces the same pre-main ASan SEGV, so memory corruption is not confirmed for the finding. Source analysis of json/internal/parser.cc shows ParseAny() buffers the full object and reparses mark.value.UpToUnread() with no size guard, which supports classifying this as a logic/resource-consumption bug rather than CONFIRMED_MEMORY.
